meta.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering

overview.thesisTitle: Development of a Scalable GIS Mapping and Predictive Analysis Framework for Urban Heat Island (UHI) Monitoring
overview.thesisSubtitle: A Micro-Analysis of Mirpur 12 with Regional Extension across Bangladesh
overview.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Engineering
overview.problemStatement: Standard meteorological stations capture broad city-wide weather patterns but fail to identify hyper-localized, block-by-block thermal risk zones. Urban planners lack an accessible, high-resolution geospatial tool capable of fusing ground-truth observations with remote sensing indices to dynamically predict and mitigate thermal anomalies in dense sectors such as Mirpur 12, Dhaka.
objectives: Develop an interactive GIS application mapping the localized thermal profile of Mirpur 12 using GPS field survey and derived spectral indices.
Engineer a modular, scalable framework extending macro-analysis to Dhaka Metropolitan Area, Sylhet, Rajshahi, and Chittagong.
Deploy a hybrid machine learning architecture forecasting multi-year urban temperature progression up to 2030.
overview.significance: The platform equips municipal authorities and researchers with evidence-based UHI intelligence—linking vegetation density to surface cooling, ranking heat hotspots, simulating green interventions, and projecting decadal thermal trajectories for sustainable urban planning in Bangladesh.

uhiExplanation.title: Urban Heat Island (UHI) Effect
uhiExplanation.summary: Urban Heat Island refers to the phenomenon where built-up areas experience significantly higher surface and air temperatures than adjacent rural or vegetated surroundings.
causes: Concrete, asphalt, and buildings absorb and store solar radiation (high thermal mass, low albedo).
Reduced vegetation limits evapotranspiration and shading that naturally cool the environment.
Traffic, industry, and human activity add anthropogenic heat to the urban canopy layer.
Urban geometry traps long-wave radiation, restricting nocturnal cooling.
impacts: Elevated public health risk during heatwaves
Increased energy demand for air conditioning
Ecological stress on urban flora and fauna
Amplified inequality in thermally vulnerable neighbourhoods

methodology.title: Research Methodology
methodology.subtitle: End-to-end pipeline from satellite-ready inputs to decision-support dashboards

steps.title: Satellite Data Collection
steps.description: Landsat 8/9 thermal infrared and multispectral band parameters integrated via surface reflectance presets and regional observation templates, fused with ground survey coordinates.

steps.title: Data Preprocessing
steps.description: GPS-tagged field records and ~11,000 historical micro-observations cleaned, merged, and normalized through Pandas preprocessing scripts into unified regional datasets.

steps.title: NDVI Computation
steps.description: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index calculated as (NIR − Red) / (NIR + Red) to quantify vegetation density and canopy cooling potential.

steps.title: LST Extraction
steps.description: Land Surface Temperature derived from ground measurements and prescribed LST models, establishing per-point thermal profiles across surface types.

steps.title: Regression Analysis
steps.description: Linear regression LST = α − (β × NDVI) with Decision Tree and Random Forest comparators; R², RMSE, and MAE validate the inverse vegetation–temperature relationship.

steps.title: Heat Risk Calculation
steps.description: Composite Heat Risk Index (HRI) combining temperature, NDVI deficit, traffic density, and surface type into normalized exposure scores per monitoring point.

steps.title: Dashboard Visualization
steps.description: Flask API serves regional intelligence to a Leaflet.js command center with layer toggles, Chart.js analytics, simulation tools, and thesis-ready reporting modules.

layers.title: Satellite Data
layers.description: Landsat 8/9 bands, NDVI/LST presets, regional GeoJSON boundaries, ground-truth CSV archives

layers.title: Processing Layer
layers.description: main.py pipeline — preprocessing, index calculation, ML training, graph generation

layers.title: Analytics Layer
layers.description: Heat risk, climate score, regression, hotspots, hybrid forecast, EDSS policy engine

layers.title: Flask API
layers.description: 17 REST endpoints serving JSON, PDF reports, and regional intelligence bundles

layers.title: Leaflet Dashboard
layers.description: 8-page web platform — Command Center, GIS, Regression, Analytics, Simulation, EDSS, Reports, Alerts

findings.title: High Temperature in Concrete Zones
findings.detail: Mirpur 12 concrete corridors (Metro Rail station, Ceramic Road) recorded surface temperatures up to 32.5°C — approximately 5–6°C above adjacent vegetated pockets.

findings.title: Lower Temperature in Vegetated Zones
findings.detail: Vegetated sites such as Mirpur DOHS and Mirpur-12 Kids Park maintained cooler profiles near 26.5–29.5°C, demonstrating evapotranspiration and canopy shading effects.

findings.title: Negative NDVI–LST Correlation
findings.detail: Linear regression on Mirpur 12 ground truth yielded R² = 0.859 with Pearson r ≈ −0.93, confirming the thesis inverse relationship between vegetation density and land surface temperature.

findings.title: Multi-City Scalability
findings.detail: Parameterized regional presets extended the identical pipeline to Dhaka, Sylhet, Rajshahi, and Chittagong — each producing independent regression metrics, heat maps, and forecast outputs.

futureWork.title: IoT Temperature Sensors
futureWork.description: Deploy street-level sensor networks for continuous ground-truth validation and live dashboard feeds.

futureWork.description: Fuse Bangladesh Meteorological Department and global forecast APIs for dynamic alert thresholds.

futureWork.description: Citizen-facing heat risk app with location-aware warnings and greening recommendations.

futureWork.title: Advanced Machine Learning
futureWork.description: Deep learning on satellite rasters, XGBoost ensembles, and spatial autocorrelation models.

futureWork.title: Crowdsourced Reporting
futureWork.description: Community heat perception reports with photo geotagging to augment official monitoring grids.
